### What is an Augmented Matrix?

An augmented matrix is a compact way to represent a system of linear equations. It combines the coefficients of the variables and the constants from the right-hand side of the equations into a single matrix. The left part of the matrix contains the coefficients of the variables, and the rightmost column (separated by a line) contains the constants.
